creative_brief: Split-screen image: left side shows Rachel Ward on her regenerative farm with cattle in green pastures; right side shows a family collecting fresh groceries from a modern FarmThru hub.
headline: The day Rachel watched 20 years of farming burn to the ground
subhead: Then she discovered what the supermarkets were really paying her for beef
hero_copy: Rachel Ward spent two decades building Rachel's Farm on the NSW coast. Regenerative pastures. Grass-fed cattle. Then the bushfires came. What nearly broke her wasn't the flames — it was learning the supermarkets sold her premium beef for $45/kg while paying her $3.

sections.heading: The farm that refused to give up
sections.body: Rachel rebuilt. But this time, she asked a different question: what if families could buy direct from the farm? No wholesalers taking their cut. No 2,400km journeys in cold storage. Just beef that's days old, not weeks old, from pastures you can actually visit.

sections.heading: From Rachel's farm to your nearest hub
sections.body: That's how FarmThru started. Rachel's grass-fed beef now goes direct to Sydney families via our Brookvale hub. Along with wild-caught salmon from Collins on the South Coast. Heritage pork from Bundarra Farm. Eggs from Farmer Brown. All 100% regenerative. All collected fresh from your local hub, not delivered after weeks in a warehouse.

sections.heading: Own a piece of the change
sections.body: We're preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign so families like yours can own part of FarmThru. Join the waitlist with a $5 refundable deposit and you'll be first to invest when we launch on Birchal. Because the people eating the food should own the system that brings it to them.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.
